context_pack.company_context: Ernst & Young (EY) is a multinational professional services network headquartered in London, England. It is one of the largest professional services networks in the world, known for offering assurance, tax, consulting, and advisory services. EY is committed to building a better working world through their services and community engagements.
context_pack.product_context: The focus is on qualifying for an O1-A visa as an instructional designer, which involves showcasing extraordinary ability in the education field. Key differentiators include demonstrating a record of exceptional achievement, recognition in the field, and contributing significantly to the development of educational technologies and methodologies.
context_pack.industry_context: The instructional design industry is increasingly integrating technology, focusing on e-learning solutions and digital content development due to the rise of remote and hybrid learning models post-COVID-19.

terminology_glossary.O1-A Visa: A type of U.S. visa for individuals who possess extraordinary ability in their field of expertise.
terminology_glossary.Instructional Designer: A professional who creates instructional materials and experiences to facilitate learning and improve performance.
terminology_glossary.Learning Management System (LMS): A software application for the administration, documentation, tracking, reporting, and delivery of educational courses or training programs.
terminology_glossary.Project-Based Learning (PBL): An instructional methodology that encourages students to learn by engaging them in real-world and meaningful projects.

typical_workflows.traditional_process: Traditionally, instructional designers focus on curriculum development, instructional material creation, and student assessment. Optimized workflows with advanced LMS such as GoTracker involve leveraging AI for personalized learning experiences, tracking long-term project goals, and providing data-driven insights for performance improvement.

uncertainties: [uncertain: specific steps to qualify for O1-A as an instructional designer]
[uncertain: exact criteria for 'extraordinary ability' in educational technology]

story_brief.user_inputs:
How to qualify O1-A as an instructional designer. Please search online relevant information and reference the resources I attached.

--- Reference Materials ---

[File: O1 alignment table_Gabrielle Qianhui Sun.pdf] Table

1: Award Organization Content Evidence Learning Guild

30 Under 30 (2025) Learning Guild

Founded in 2002, the Learning Guild is the largest publisher/event organizer in the Learning Industry.

The 30 Under 30 program is to recognize emerging leaders in this field

Proving Ground Agentic Hackathon (sponsored by 500 Global, SAP, Arize, Toolhouse, etc.)

1/29 teams (150 selected builders)

May 2025, Palo Alto, CA

Cascadian JS hackathon Best use of Langflow award (out of 24 teams)

Sept 2025, Seattle, WA

EY ‘WOW award’ Employee awards

18 times NSF I-Corps™ Fund project: GoTracker - A Learning Management System specifically designed for Project-based Learning

About this initiative:

Qianhui led a multi-disciplinary team of researchers, designers, and developers to design and develop GoTracker, a Learning Management System (LMS) that addresses two critical issues in Project-based Learning: Students lose track of goals in long-term projects and less data to give deterministic scoring.

My role: Team lead/”CEO” - Leadership: Led a multi-disciplinary team of researchers, designers, and developers to design and develop GoTracker.

- Innovation: There are a ton of LMSs out there, but we were the only one that focused on Project-based Learning (PBL). [Exhibit 2.1-1] Organization Content Evidence

- Impact: Addressed the lack of LMS solutions focused on project-based learning, a vital component for K-12 educational entities.

Media Coverage Carnegie Mellon University features us in an article: “HCI Class Project Turned Entrepreneurship Lesson for Dedicated METALS Students,” showcasing our entrepreneurial spirit. We were the finalist of the McGinnis Venture Competition.

[Exhibit 2.1-2] End user’s interview Custom discovery interviews with experts validated the criticality of the problems being solved and the effectiveness of our solution.

Adam Kronebusch Mathematics Department chair, Central Catholic High School, Pittsburgh, PA

Anne Fay Director of eLearning at Winchester Thurston School, Pittsburgh, PA

[Exhibit 2.1-3]

Table 2. Judging other people’s work

Name of the awards/peer-reviewed conferences Significance of the work and my role Evidence

CODiE award

Significance of the work: Since 1986, the SIIA CODiE Awards have honored thousands of software, education, information, and media products, leaders, and teams for achieving excellence. Over the years the CODiE Awards have built a strong reputation as the leading peer-recognized program in the Business and Education Technology industries. Each CODiE Award win serves as incredible market validation for a product’s innovation, vision, and overall industry impact. To ensure the very best products make it to the winner's circle, each nomination receives a detailed review by two expert Judges during the first round Expert review; each finalist product receives a second peer review by a team of industry leaders.

My role:

My role is to evaluate 5 companies in the Education Technology Product & Leadership Category, including some of the most prestigious EdTech companies in the world (e.g., Alef Pathway Education - $515 million IPO valued at $2.5 billion, Pearson - established 1844, Power School - Acquired by BainCapital at $5.6 billion)

[Exhibit 2.2-1]

MEDEA award Significance of the work:

The MEDEA Awards was launched in November 2007 and organized by the Media & Learning Association. It aims to encourage innovation in the use of moving images and sound in education, across Europe and beyond.

My role:

I was selected as a judge in 2024 and reviewed 5 media entries from various higher education institutions, ranging from VR to video.

[Exhibit 2.2-2]

Learning ideas conference Significance of the work: The Learning Ideas Conference brings together researchers and practitioners from around the world, welcoming anyone interested in the uses of technology to improve education and workplace learning. It is sponsored by Kaleidoscope Learning and the International E-Learning Association. The Conference Chair is David Guralnick, who is also an adjunct professor at Columbia University since 2005.

My role:

I have been serving as the conference chair from 2022 to the present. I serve as a virtual breakout moderator and scholarly paper reviewer.

[Exhibit 2.2-3]

CHI (Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems) Significance of the work:

ACM CHI is generally considered the most prestigious in the field of human-computer interaction (ranked No.1 impact score according to Google Scholar) and is one of the top-ranked conferences in computer science. CHI has been held annually since 1982 and attracts thousands of international attendees.

My role:

I was a peer reviewer of two submissions related to education technology and AI in learning in 2024.

[Exhibit 2.2-4]

NFTE - Network for Teaching Entrepreneurship

Significance of the work: Network for Teaching Entrepreneurship (NFTE) is a global non-profit organization that provides high-quality entrepreneurship education to middle and high school adults. This competition aims to help some of America’s most talented young people get more guidance on their entrepreneurial journey.

My role:

Serving as a judge, I provided my feedback on 5 US high school students’ business plans in terms of the originality of the ideas, technical feasibility, business model, and risks.

[Exhibit 2.2-5]

Direct research journal of education

Significance of the work:

[Exhibit 2.2-6]

and vocational studies (ISSN: 2734-2174 | DOI: 10.26765/DRJEVS) Direct Research Journal of Education and Vocational Studies (DRJEVS) publishes papers in the following areas Educational Research, Social Sciences Education (Economics Education, Geography Education, Political Science Education, Account Education, Social Studies Education), Language Education (Arabic, English, French, Mother Tongue), Arts Education (History, Christian religion, Islamic Religion) and Foundations of Education (History of Education, Comparative Education, Philosophy of Education, Educational Psychology, Nursery and Primary Education, Sociology of Education).

All papers submitted to the journal will be peer-reviewed.

My role:

Invited talk 1: How to transform physical classroom learning materials into virtual classroom learning materials, delivered Jan 19, 2023, at online.edu

Original contribution

-

As COVID led to the transformation of learning delivery methods from offline to online, hybrid work mode also poses new challenges to learning teams as they need to accommodate people joining from onsite and [Exhibit 2.3-1]

those dialing in remotely.

-

Qianhui’s work is pioneering in experimenting ways that could ultimately lead to a better learning experience in this new working mode.

Major significance

-

The organization that invited Qianhui to the talk, online.edu, is a well-established online education publisher in China, dating back to the 1980s, dedicated to digitalizing learning experiences.

[Exhibit 2.3-2]

Invited talk 2: Design AI-powered role-play simulations, delivered Mar 19, 2024, at L&D Shakers

Original contribution

-

Role-play simulation has been an effective method for training power skills, including client communication, relationship building, sales, and leadership topics.

-

As AI gains the ability to hold multiple rounds of conversations, leveraging AI to scale the capacity of human facilitators and democratize this training experience has been a worthy practice.

-

Qianhui’s work is adapted from a previously designed simulation. She designed the system prompt and wrote the code.

[Exhibit 2.3-3]

Major significance

-

L&D Shaker, the organization that invited Qianhui for a talk, is an award-winning online learning community with over 4,000 audience globally.

-

Positive public feedback shared by participants publicly, who are also L&D practitioners.
